results.potential_vulnerability: Reads environment variables and exfiltrates them over HTTP
evidence: lib/telemetry.js collects sensitive environment variables (NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, CI_JOB_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) and reads credential files (.npmrc, .gitconfig, .pypirc)
index.js calls collectConnectivityHints() and encodes the collected hints in base64
index.js sends the payload via HTTP POST to 127.0.0.1:9999/telemetry without user consent or disclosure
results.rationale: The package reads and exfiltrates sensitive credentials and environment variables over HTTP. The data collection is covert (error handlers suppress failures), triggered on module load, and sent to a hardcoded endpoint. This constitutes confirmed network exfiltration of secrets.
